title: Fix _build_edge_dict uuid fallback semantics and add missing test coverage
analysis: Task 131 extracted _build_edge_dict as a shared helper but introduced a semantic regression in _search_graphiti at line 731. The original code used `getattr(edge, 'uuid', str(i))` which only fell back to `str(i)` when the uuid attribute was entirely absent. The refactored code uses `d['uuid'] or str(i)` which falls back for ANY falsy uuid value (empty string, 0, False), not just None. Since _build_edge_dict returns None for missing uuid attrs (via `getattr(e, 'uuid', None)`) and MockEdge defaults uuid to '' (empty string), this is a real behavioral difference: an edge with an explicitly empty-string uuid now gets silently replaced with its enumeration index. The fix is straightforward: change `or` to an explicit `is not None` check — `d['uuid'] if d['uuid'] is not None else str(i)`. Additionally, TestBuildEdgeDict has four coverage gaps: (1) no test for invalid_at-only temporal dict, (2) test_minimal_edge_returns_safe_defaults doesn't assert uuid/fact/name values, (3) no integration-level test proving empty-string uuid flows correctly through _search_graphiti, and (4) no test for non-string episode objects being str()-converted in provenance. All four are addressed in this plan.

steps.description: Write failing test `test_empty_string_uuid_preserved_in_search_graphiti` in TestSearch class (test_memory_service.py). Create a MockEdge with uuid='' (the default), mock service.graphiti.search to return it via AsyncMock, mock service.mem0.search to return empty results, call `await service.search(query='test', project_id='test')`, and assert `results[0].id == ''`. This test will FAIL against current code because `'' or str(0)` evaluates to '0', not ''.

Fix uuid fallback in _search_graphiti (memory_service.py line 731). Change `id=d['uuid'] or str(i)` to `id=d['uuid'] if d['uuid'] is not None else str(i)`. This preserves original getattr semantics: only fall back when uuid is None (attribute absent from edge), not for empty strings or other falsy values. Run step-1 test to confirm it passes.

Extend `test_minimal_edge_returns_safe_defaults` in TestBuildEdgeDict (test_memory_service.py ~line 774). Add three assertions after the existing ones: `assert d['uuid'] == ''` (MockEdge default is empty string), `assert d['fact'] == 'bare fact'`, and `assert d['name'] is None`. These complete the coverage of all six dict keys for the minimal edge case.

Add `test_edge_with_only_invalid_at_returns_temporal_dict` in TestBuildEdgeDict, placed after the existing valid_at-only test (~line 794). Create MockEdge with fact='fact', uuid='u-inv', invalid_at='2026-06-01T00:00:00+00:00' (valid_at defaults to None). Assert temporal is not None, temporal['valid_at'] is None, temporal['invalid_at'] == '2026-06-01T00:00:00+00:00'. Mirrors the existing valid_at-only test for symmetry.

Add `test_non_string_episode_objects_converted_to_str_in_provenance` in TestBuildEdgeDict, placed after the last existing test (~line 841). Define an inline edge class (following the EdgeWithoutUuid/EdgeWithoutFact pattern) with an episodes list containing objects that have a custom __str__ returning 'custom-ep-repr'. Assert d['provenance'] == ['custom-ep-repr']. This verifies the `[str(ep) for ep in episodes]` logic handles non-string objects correctly.

design_decisions.decision: Use `is not None` check instead of truthiness for uuid fallback
design_decisions.rationale: The original `getattr(edge, 'uuid', str(i))` only used the fallback when the attribute was missing entirely (i.e., getattr returned the default). The _build_edge_dict helper returns None for missing uuid attrs (via `getattr(e, 'uuid', None)`). So `is not None` exactly preserves the original semantics: empty strings, 0, False are all valid uuid values that should not trigger fallback.

design_decisions.decision: Test the uuid fix at _search_graphiti integration level via service.search(), not directly on _build_edge_dict
design_decisions.rationale: The bug is in how _search_graphiti CONSUMES the dict from _build_edge_dict (the `or` expression), not in _build_edge_dict itself. Testing via service.search() matches the existing pattern (TestSearch class, e.g., test_search_graphiti_results_get_inferred_category_when_single_primary at line 429) and exercises the actual code path where the regression occurs.

design_decisions.decision: Use inline custom class for non-string episode test instead of modifying MockEdge
design_decisions.rationale: MockEdge.episodes is typed as list[str] which makes it awkward to pass non-string objects. An inline edge class (following EdgeWithoutUuid/EdgeWithoutFact patterns already in TestBuildEdgeDict) gives full control over the episodes attribute type without changing shared fixtures.

design_decisions.decision: Coverage-only tests (steps 3-5) are pure additions that pass against existing code
design_decisions.rationale: The existing _build_edge_dict code correctly handles invalid_at-only temporal, minimal edge defaults, and non-string episode str() conversion. These tests fill documentation/coverage gaps — they don't drive new implementation. Only step 1 (uuid regression) is a true red-green TDD cycle.

reuse.how: Import from tests.conftest for standard edge construction in steps 1, 3, 4. MockEdge has uuid='' default which is central to the uuid regression test (step 1).

Reuse the existing fixture for all tests. For the _search_graphiti integration test (step 1), override service.graphiti.search with AsyncMock returning a MockEdge list, following the pattern at line 436.
Follow the established pattern of defining edge classes inline within test methods for cases where MockEdge's type constraints don't fit (step 5: non-string episodes).

Add the uuid regression test (step 1) in TestSearch alongside existing tests like test_search_graphiti_results_get_inferred_category_when_single_primary, following the same mock setup and assertion pattern.
